message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Summary</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>Genome sequences constitute the primary evidence on the origin and spread of the 2019-2020 Covid-19 pandemic. Rapid comparative analysis of coronavirus SARS-CoV-2 genomes is critical for disease control, outbreak forecasting, and developing clinical interventions. CoV Genome Tracker is a web portal dedicated to trace Covid-19 outbreaks in real time using a haplotype network, an accurate and scalable representation of genomic changes in a rapidly evolving population. We resolve the direction of mutations by using a bat-associated genome as outgroup. At a broader evolutionary time scale, a companion browser provides gene-by-gene and codon-by-codon evolutionary rates to facilitate the search for molecular targets of clinical interventions.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Availability and Implementation</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    CoV Genome Tracker is publicly available at
                    <jats:ext-link xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" ext-link-type="uri" xlink:href="http://cov.genometracker.org">http://cov.genometracker.org</jats:ext-link>
                    and updated weekly with the data downloaded from GISAID (
                    <jats:ext-link xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" ext-link-type="uri" xlink:href="http://gisaid.org">http://gisaid.org</jats:ext-link>
                    ). The website is implemented with a custom JavaScript script based on jQuery (
                    <jats:ext-link xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" ext-link-type="uri" xlink:href="https://jquery.com">https://jquery.com</jats:ext-link>
                    ) and D3-force (
                    <jats:ext-link xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" ext-link-type="uri" xlink:href="https://github.com/d3/d3-force">https://github.com/d3/d3-force</jats:ext-link>
                    ).
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Contact</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    <jats:email>weigang@genectr.hunter.cuny.edu</jats:email>
                    , City University of New York, Hunter College
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Supplementary Information</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>
                    All supporting scripts developed in JavaScript, Python, BASH, and PERL programming languages are available as Open Source at the GitHub repository
                    <jats:ext-link xmlns:xlink="http://www.w3.org/1999/xlink" ext-link-type="uri" xlink:href="https://github.com/weigangq/cov-browser">https://github.com/weigangq/cov-browser</jats:ext-link>
                    .
                  </jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: CoV Genome Tracker: tracing genomic footprints of Covid-19 pandemic
